text: The optimal ACWR range of 0.8-1.3 is associated with the lowest injury incidence (56%), compared to higher rates outside this range.
Tissue structure injuries have the highest injury incidence (79%) across ACWR-related injuries, suggesting soft tissue is most vulnerable to workload spikes.
Lower extremity injuries account for 73% of ACWR-related injuries, making leg injury monitoring a priority when tracking acute-to-chronic workload ratios.
External load metrics alone show a lower injury incidence (64%) compared to combined internal and external load metrics (69%), suggesting multi-metric monitoring may capture more injury risk.
Athletes over age 25 show a 73% injury incidence in ACWR-related analyses, indicating older athletes may need more conservative workload progression.

ACWR should be used with caution as a sole workload measurement tool due to heterogeneity in calculation methods (e.g., rolling average vs. exponentially weighted) and potential publication bias across studies.
